title: dryopteris mucronata
intro: Dryopteris mucronata, also known as the Japanese wood fern, is a species of fern belonging to the genus Dryopteris. It is native to East Asia, particularly in China, Japan, and the Korean Peninsula. Dryopteris mucronata has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. It can be prepared as a tea, capsule, or tincture, and its health benefits include reducing stress, improving digestion, and supporting immune function.
